data.title: Chevron flies Venezuela staff to oil sites despite U.S. warning - Bloomberg
data.description:
Chevron (CVX) is flying employees from Caracas to its oil production sites in Venezuela despite a warning from the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration that air travel in the region may be unsafe due to military satellite interference, Bloomberg reported Thursday.

Chevron (CVX) - the only U.S. oil producer still operating in Venezuela - reportedly sent employees Monday to its facilities around the country, part of the company's routine weekly oversight of oil ventures that pump ~25% of Venezuela’s crude output.

"Chevron's operations in Venezuela continue in full compliance with laws and regulations applicable to its business, as well as the sanctions frameworks provided for by the U.S. government," a company spokesperson said.

The safety of Caribbean airspace has become an issue as the Trump administration continues its military buildup in the region, and coincides with a surge in communication jamming that has affected satellite navigation and other systems in the area, making air travel risky, although most domestic commercial and private flights have maintained operations under threat from the Maduro government.

The Trump administration has barred most U.S. companies from doing business in Venezuela, but it allows Chevron (CVX) to maintain minimal maintenance on equipment in its joint ventures and to keep exporting Venezuelan oil to the U.S.

data.title: IDEX Corp names new CFO
data.description:
• IDEX (IEX) appointed Sean M. Gillen as senior vice president and chief financial officer, effective January 5, 2026.
  • Interim CFO Akhil Mahendra will return to his prior role as vice president of Corporate Development.
  • Gillen brings nearly seven years of CFO experience from AAR CORP. (AIR), along with prior roles at USG Corporation and Goldman Sachs.

data.title: Cooper Cos. jumps after starting strategic review, naming new board chair
data.description:
Cooper Cos. (COO) soared 12% in after hours trading after initiating a strategic review of its businesses and naming a new board chair.

Cooper (COO) is undergoing a formal and comprehensive strategic review of ts businesses, corporate structure, strategy, operations, and capital allocation priorities, according to a statement on Thursday. The company will look at options including  partnerships, joint ventures, divestitures, mergers, business combinations, and other transactions.

The company named Colleen Jay as chairman to succeed Robert Weiss, effective Jan. 2. 

The business review comes after it was reported in October that activist investor Jana Partners took a stake and planned to push the company to review its strategic alternatives. Jana wants Cooper to combine its contact-lens unit with rival Bausch + Lomb (BLCO), the WSJ reported. 

During its strategic review, the company will focus on repurchasing shares under its recently announced $2 billion share repurchase program.

Cooper Cos. (COO) also reported Q3 results on Thursday.

data.title: Goldman Sachs, Carlyle, StepStone to provide $10K private fund access on Willow
data.description:
Goldman Sachs (GS), Carlyle Group (CG) and StepStone (STEP) have agreed to offer accredited users of alternative investment platform Willow Wealth access to private credit funds with minimum investments as low as $10K, Willow said on Thursday.

With the deal, Willow users will be able to invest directly in the Carlyle Tactical Private Credit Fund, the Goldman Sachs Real Estate Diversified Income Fund, and StepStone Private Markets, Bloomberg reported.

The three funds, collectively representing more than $11.7B in assets under management as of Sept. 30, 2025, aim to give investors immediate exposure to deployed portfolios across private credit, private equity, and real estate in a structure designed to provide periodic liquidity

The move underscores the $1.7T private credit industry's growing push into retail markets to meet investor demand. Willow isn't the only platform offering access to low-cost private credit. For instance, Titan Financial provides a $2K minimum initial investment on the Carlyle Tactical Private Credit Fund, its website said.

“Private markets are undergoing a shift in how they’re accessed and who can access them,” said Willow Wealth CEO Mitchell Caplan. “Today’s announcement represents our vision for the next generation of private markets investing — one where investors can build complete portfolios combining always-on core exposure through evergreen funds with targeted, differentiated individual opportunities.

data.title: Apple shuffles executive ranks again, as Adams and Jackson set to depart
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) made changes to its executive ranks again this week, as the company said on Thursday that its long-time general counsel and head of environmental policy will depart the iPhone maker. 

Kate Adams, who had been Apple's general counsel since 2017, will be replaced by Jennifer Newstead, who will take over the job on March 1. Newstead, who was most recently chief legal officer at Meta (META) and previously served as the legal adviser of the U.S. Department of State, will report directly to CEO Tim Cook and serve on its executive team.

“We couldn’t be more pleased to have Jennifer join our team,” said Cook in a statement. “She brings an extraordinary depth of experience and skill to the role, and will advance Apple’s important work all over the world. We are also pleased that Jennifer will be overseeing both the Legal and Government Affairs organizations, given the increasing overlap between the work of both teams and her substantial background in international affairs. I know she will be an excellent leader going forward.”

“I have long admired Apple’s deep focus on innovation and strong commitment to its values, its customers, and to making the world a better place,” Newstead added. “I am honored to join the company and to lead an extraordinary team who are dedicated each and every day to doing what’s in the best interest of Apple’s users.”

Lisa Jackson, who was Apple's vice president for Environment, Policy, and Social Initiatives, will retire in late January 2026. The Government Affairs organization will transition to Adams, until her retirement. It will then be led by Newstead.

“I am deeply appreciative of Lisa's contributions,” Cook added. “She has been instrumental in helping us reduce our global greenhouse emissions by more than 60 percent compared to 2015 levels. She has also been a critical strategic partner in engaging governments around the world, advocating for the best interests of our users on a myriad of topics, as well as advancing our values, from education and accessibility to privacy and security.”

Prior to joining Apple in 2013, Jackson was the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency under President Obama. 

Earlier this week, Apple announced that John Giannandrea, senior vice president for Machine Learning and AI Strategy, would step down from his position and will serve as an advisor to the company before retiring in the spring of 2026. He will be replaced by artificial intelligence researcher Amar Subramanya, who will join Apple from Microsoft (MSFT).

On Wednesday, Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg announced that his company had hired Alan Dye, who served as the head of Apple's user interface design team for a decade, in a new role as chief design officer. He will be replaced at Apple with Steve Lemay.

data.title: Mobilicom to replace ADSs with ordinary shares on Nasdaq from Dec. 8
data.description:
• Mobilicom (MOB) on Thursday said it will transition its American Depositary Shares to a direct listing of its ordinary shares on the Nasdaq Capital Market, effective at the market open on December 8.
  • The ADR program will terminate after market close on December 5, alongside a 1-for-275 reverse share split.
  • Each ADS will be cancelled and converted into one ordinary share, with no action required by holders.
  • Mobilicom said the move is intended to simplify its structure, cut administrative costs and improve U.S. investor access, including potential eligibility for indices and ETFs that exclude ADRs.
  • MOB -0.0% after hours to $7.43.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Darling Ingredients announces agreement to sell about $60M in production tax credits
data.description:
• Darling Ingredients (DAR) on Thursday announced an agreement to sell approximately $60 million of production tax credits to a corporate buyer.
  • These credits were generated under the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) by the company’s Diamond Green Diesel joint venture. The proceeds of the sale are scheduled to be received by Dec. 31, 2025, upon satisfaction of certain funding conditions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: PTC appoints new CFO
data.description:
• PTC (PTC) named Jennifer DiRico as executive vice president and chief financial officer, effective January 1, 2026.
  • DiRico succeeds outgoing CFO Kristian Talvitie, who will remain in the role through December 31, 2025.
  • DiRico brings experience from Commvault, where she currently serves as CFO.
  • PTC reaffirmed its full fiscal year 2026 and Q1 guidance previously issued on November 5, 2025.

data.title: Ulta raises outlook after strong Q3 while higher debt tempers the glow
data.description:
Ulta Beauty (ULTA) crushed Wall Street’s expectations for the third quarter with a solid top- and bottom-line beat and comparable store sales that were nearly double expectations, driving shares higher in after-hours trading.

“Exciting assortment newness, improved in-store and digital experiences, and bold marketing efforts are resonating with our guests and drove strong sales results, market share gains, and growth across all categories and channels, with notable strength in ecommerce,” said Ulta CEO Kecia Steelman.

To reflect the strong third quarter and to incorporate the company’s expectation for a successful holiday season, Ulta Beauty (ULTA) raised its full year guidance again. The company now sees sales to be ~$12.3B, up from prior guidance of $12.0B to $12.1B, and better than $12.06B estimates, with comparable store sales expected to increase 4.4% to 4.7% from +2.5% to +3.5%, previously.

Profitability is also expected to be better than initially expected with EPS now seen between $25.20 and $25.50 per share from $23.85 and $24.30 per share, above the $24.57 estimate. Operating margin guidance was raised to a range of 12.3% to 12.4% from 11.9% to 12.0%.

For the reported quarter, increased traffic, new stores, and the acquisition of Space NK drove sales up 13% to a better-than-expected $2.9B. Comparable store sales jumped 6.3% from 0.6% a year ago and beat 3.41% estimates.

On the bottom line, Ulta (ULTA) earned an adjusted profit of $5.14 per share, unchanged from the same quarter last year, but $0.53 better than anticipated. And reflecting lower inventory shrink and a higher margin on merchandise, gross profit margin increased 70 basis points to 40.4%.

The balance sheet, however, took a hit. To support share repurchases, capital expenditures, and “ongoing capital allocation priorities,” the company fell further into debt with short-term debt swelling nearly 3x to $551.7M. During the quarter, Ulta (ULTA) repurchased 426,914 shares at a cost of $224.7M, opened 28 new stores, and remodeled 15 existing stores.

data.title: USA Rare Earth rips higher after supply deal with Compass Diversified, Solvay
data.description:
USA Rare Earth (USAR) closed +24.6% on Thursday after announcing earlier that its Less Common Metals subsidiary signed an agreement to supply European chemical company Solvay (OTCPK:SVYSF) (OTCPK:SLVYY) and Compass Diversified (CODI) subsidiary Arnold Magnetic Technologies with high-quality rare earth materials for Arnold's production of advanced permanent magnets; financial details were not provided.

The agreement comes less than a month after USA Rare Earth (USAR) closed its acquisition of Less Common Metals for $100 million, citing LCI's unique position as the only proven ex-China producer of both light and heavy rare earth permanent magnet metals and alloys at scale at its 67,000 sq ft production facility in the U.K.

"This collaboration exemplifies the type of industrial partnerships that are rebuilding strength and resilience across the rare-earth ecosystem outside of China," USA Rare Earth (USAR) CEO Barbara Humpton said.

USA Rare Earth (USAR) shares extended gains through the afternoon as a Trump administration official said the U.S. government plans to take more equity stakes in critical minerals companies.
